evidence.source_title: Short-course antibiotics for common infections: what do we know and where do we go from here?
evidence.raw_snippet:
PubMed ID: 36075498. 2023/02/01 00:00. BACKGROUND: Over the past 25 years, researchers have performed >120 randomized 
controlled trials (RCTs) illustrating short courses to be non-inferior to long 
courses of antibiotics for common bacterial infections.
OBJECTIVE: We sought to determine whether clinical data from RCTs affirm the 
mantra of 'shorter is better' for antibiotic durations in 7 common infections: 
pneumonia, urinary tract infection, intra-abdominal infection, bacteraemia, skin 
and s
